content.text: 12 columns need ALTER from TINYINT/DATE/INT to VARCHAR to store string values:

Columns already VARCHAR (no schema change needed): avetta_approved, atc_approved_verification_record, crown_approved_verification_record, sba_approved_verification_record, input_into_sm, payment_terms, safety_status, type, type_of_company.

- Update setupColumnDefs() for 21 columns: add editable:true, cellEditor:'agSelectCellEditor', cellEditorParams with dropdown options. Remove vendorDateEditor from 3 columns, remove vendorCheckboxRenderer from 6 columns.
- Update form controls to match new input types (dropdowns replace checkboxes/date pickers). Update convertCheckbox/convertForSave logic.

The existing update_field endpoint (vendor_admin_controller.php) already handles any field update generically — validates field name against DB schema and saves whatever value is passed. After DB column type changes, it will naturally accept the new string values.
/vendor-admin (Vendor Admin grid page) - inline cell editing behavior changes
(aps_vendor.php) - No code changes needed. Model reads/writes fields generically.
POST /vendor_admin/update_field - No code change needed; already handles string values
POST /vendor_admin/vendor_list - No change; returns data as-is from DB
POST /vendor_admin/create - May need minor update to default values for altered columns
GET /vendor_admin/distinct_values/{column} - No change; auto-returns new string values
Create SQL migration: ALTER 12 columns from TINYINT/DATE/INT to VARCHAR
Onboarding Doc Request, VRF Acknowledgement, W-9 Received Config: editable:true, cellEditor:'agSelectCellEditor', values:['','YES','NO']
COI Received, COI Endorsements, ATC Approved, Crown Approved, SBA Approved, Diversity Status, Input to SM Config: editable:true, cellEditor:'agSelectCellEditor', values:['','YES','NO','NA']
MSA Status, GC Approved, Safety Cart Review, Safety Status Config: editable:true, cellEditor:'agSelectCellEditor', values:['','Approved','Pending','NA']
PO Half Year Status Config: editable:true, cellEditor:'agSelectCellEditor', values:['','Active','Inactive']
Avetta Approved, Type, Date Sent, Certificate Expiration, Payment Terms, Type of Company Config: editable:true (uses default AG Grid text editor)
Blank ('') as first option in all dropdowns satisfies AC5 (default blank, revert to blank).
Remove cellRenderer:'vendorCheckboxRenderer' from: coi_endorsements, diversity_status, vendor_msa_status, gc_approved_to_work_from_sourcing, safety_cert_review_complete, po_half_year_status
Remove cellEditor:'vendorDateEditor' from: onboarding_document_request, vrf_acknowledgement_received, coi_received
Remove filter:'agDateColumnFilter' from those 3 columns, replace with agSetColumnFilter
Replace checkbox/toggle inputs with select dropdowns for dropdown columns
Replace date picker inputs with text inputs for free-form date columns
Update boolean conversion functions (no longer 0/1, now string values)

Reuses the existing AG Grid agSelectCellEditor pattern already proven on the Flag and Status columns (lines 308-331 of vendors-admin.component.ts). The save mechanism (onCellValueChanged -> POST update_field) already handles string values and provides optimistic UI feedback (green/red cell flash). No new components or renderers needed.
3 columns → YES/NO dropdown (Onboarding Doc Request, VRF Acknowledgement, W9 Received)
7 columns → YES/NO/NA dropdown (COI Received, COI Endorsements, ATC Approved, Crown Approved, SBA Approved, Diversity Status, Input to SM)
4 columns → Approved/Pending/NA dropdown (MSA Status, GC Approved, Safety Cart Review, Safety Status)
1 column → Active/Inactive dropdown (PO Half Year Status)
6 columns → Free-form text (Avetta Approved, Type, Date Sent, Certificate Expiration, Payment Terms, Type of Company)
Checkboxes and date pickers replaced with mat-select dropdowns for dropdown columns
Date fields (Date Sent, Certificate Expiration) converted to free-form text inputs
All dropdowns include blank option for revert-to-blank (AC5)
12 columns altered from TINYINT/DATE/INT to VARCHAR to support string values
This SQL must be manually executed against the test server database before testing. It is not auto-applied on deployment.
Updated create action defaults for date_sent and certificate_expiration (0 → empty string)
each column in the Vendor Admin Table must be configured as follows:
ATC Approved Verification Received or N/A: Drop-down ("YES", "NO", "NA")
Crown Approved Verification Received or N/A: Drop-down ("YES", "NO", "NA")
SBA Approved Verification Received or N/A: Drop-down ("YES", "NO", "NA")
that drop-down columns only allow selection from the specified options ("YES", "NO").
that invalid or custom values cannot be entered into drop-down columns.
that free-form columns accept any text input.
that free-form columns display guidance text where expected values are specified.
that any changes made in the Vendor Admin Table UI are immediately reflected in the interface.
that changes made in the Vendor Admin Table UI persist correctly to the backend database.
that data consistency is maintained between the UI and backend after refresh or re-login.
that all drop-down and free-form columns default to a blank status when first displayed.
that users can revert a drop-down selection back to blank after choosing "YES" or "NO".
that the blank reversion behavior in drop-downs matches the functionality implemented in the Incident Admin module.
each column in the Vendor Admin Table must be configured as follows:
ATC Approved Verification Received or N/A: Drop-down ("YES", "NO", "NA")
Crown Approved Verification Received or N/A: Drop-down ("YES", "NO", "NA")
SBA Approved Verification Received or N/A: Drop-down ("YES", "NO", "NA")
that drop-down columns only allow selection from the specified options ("YES", "NO").
that invalid or custom values cannot be entered into drop-down columns.
that free-form columns accept any text input.
that free-form columns display guidance text where expected values are specified.
that any changes made in the Vendor Admin Table UI are immediately reflected in the interface.
that changes made in the Vendor Admin Table UI persist correctly to the backend database.
that data consistency is maintained between the UI and backend after refresh or re-login.
that all drop-down and free-form columns default to a blank status when first displayed.
that users can revert a drop-down selection back to blank after choosing "YES" or "NO".
that the blank reversion behavior in drop-downs matches the functionality implemented in the Incident Admin module.
All columns with yes or no drop downs should not have 0000-00-00 in the grid. It should be blank. Maybe mirror it like the Incident Admin Category drop down. See attachment.
All columns with the date should show as a blank date format. Not 0000-00-00. When you click on it, the format is mm/dd/yyyy. Once the date populates it is yyyy/mm/dd. See attachment.

Resolution: Added valueFormatter functions to sanitize legacy data display in the Vendor Admin grid. Columns with dropdown values (YES/NO, YES/NO/NA, Approved/Pending/NA, Active/Inactive) and date columns now display blank instead of 0, 0000-00-00, or null values left over from the database column type migration.
Added formatDropdownValue() helper that validates cell values against allowed options and returns blank for any invalid/legacy data (0, 0000-00-00, null, or values not in the dropdown list)
Added formatDateValue() helper that returns blank for 0000-00-00 and null date values, and displays dates in mm/dd/yyyy format to match the HTML5 date picker
Applied valueFormatter to all 15 dropdown columns and 4 date-picker columns (MSA Sent to GC, MSA Signed/AP Setup, Safety Orientation Date, COI Expiration)
All formatters use arrow functions for consistent this binding
Date columns now display in mm/dd/yyyy format instead of yyyy-mm-dd, matching the date picker editor format

Verify dropdown columns (Onboarding Doc Request, VRF Acknowledgement, W9 Received, COI Received, COI Endorsements, ATC Approved, Crown Approved, SBA Approved, Diversity Status, Input Into SM, MSA Status, GC Approved, Safety Cert Review, Safety Status, PO Half Year Status) show blank instead of 0 or 0000-00-00
Verify date columns (MSA Sent to GC, MSA Signed/AP Setup, Safety Orientation Date, COI Expiration) show blank instead of 0000-00-00
Verify populated dates display as mm/dd/yyyy (e.g. 02/25/2026)
Verify selecting a dropdown value still saves correctly
Verify selecting a date still saves correctly
Verify you can revert a dropdown back to blank by selecting the empty option
Verify the saved value persists after page refresh
Note: Database cleanup queries were also provided to clear legacy 0 and 0000-00-00 values from the aps_vendors table.
Commits: Frontend Git: SM-1032 branch, commits 67a5a1c6 and 3f1028a2, merged to develop

This is still not set up correctly. Please make the drop down boxes clear that they are drop down boxes. Similar to incident columns. Make it clear those columns/rows are a drop down without user having to click on it. Also, make the date columns/rows clear that they are dates. They shouldn’t be blank.

Resolution: Added valueFormatter functions to sanitize legacy data display in the Vendor Admin grid. Columns with dropdown values (YES/NO, YES/NO/NA, Approved/Pending/NA, Active/Inactive) and date columns now display blank instead of 0, 0000-00-00, or null values left over from the database column type migration.

Created VendorSelectRendererComponent - a custom cell renderer that displays a visible <select> dropdown element at all times for all 15 dropdown columns. Users can now see the dropdown arrow without clicking the cell first. Mirrors the pattern used in Incident Admin.

Date columns (MSA Sent to GC, MSA Signed/AP Setup, Safety Orientation Date, COI Expiration) now show mm/dd/yyyy placeholder in light gray when empty, making it clear these are date fields. Populated dates display in mm/dd/yyyy format matching the date picker editor.
Added formatDateValue() helper that returns placeholder for empty dates and displays populated dates as mm/dd/yyyy
Added dateCellStyle() helper that renders empty date placeholders in light gray
All dropdown save operations go through the parent component's updateSelectField() method for consistency with the existing checkbox/inline edit patterns
All formatters use arrow functions for consistent this binding

Verify all dropdown columns (Onboarding Doc Request, VRF Acknowledgement, W9 Received, COI Received, COI Endorsements, ATC Approved, Crown Approved, SBA Approved, Diversity Status, Input Into SM, MSA Status, GC Approved, Safety Cert Review, Safety Status, PO Half Year Status) now show a visible dropdown arrow in every cell
Verify dropdown columns show blank instead of 0 or 0000-00-00
Select a value from a dropdown - verify it saves and shows green flash
Select blank from a dropdown - verify it reverts to blank and saves
Verify empty date columns show mm/dd/yyyy in light gray text
Click a date cell, pick a date - verify it displays in mm/dd/yyyy format with normal text color
Verify populated dates display as mm/dd/yyyy (e.g. 02/25/2026)
Verify you can revert a dropdown back to blank by selecting the empty option
Verify the saved value persists after page refresh
Verify export to Excel still works correctly
Note: Database cleanup queries were also provided to clear legacy 0 and 0000-00-00 values from the aps_vendors table.
Commits: Frontend Git: SM-1032 branch, commits 67a5a1c6, 3f1028a2, and 2d22b711, merged to develop
See screen recording. The drop downs are not saving. If user chooses something from the drop down, and refreshes the screen, it is not saving what the user chose.
Follow-up fix: Dropdown values not persisting after save on test server.

Root Cause: The dropdown columns (onboarding_document_request, vrf_acknowledgement_received, wnine_received, etc.) were still using DATE column type on the test server database. When saving a string value like "YES" into a DATE column, MySQL silently converts it to "0000-00-00" — so the API returned success but the value was never actually stored correctly. The column type migration from DATE to VARCHAR had only been applied locally, not on the test server.
Ran ALTER TABLE statements on the test server to convert all 15 dropdown columns from DATE to VARCHAR(10), matching the local database schema.
Ran cleanup queries to convert leftover "0000-00-00" and "0" values to blank strings.
Cleared the CakePHP model schema cache so the backend picks up the new column types.

Long-term Fix (Pipeline Cache Clear): Added an automated cache-clearing mechanism to the GitLab CI deployment pipeline. A new backend endpoint (/deploy/clear_cache) is now called automatically after every deployment to test, stage, preprod, and production. This ensures CakePHP always picks up database schema changes without manual server intervention. This is a permanent infrastructure improvement that benefits all future tickets.
Backend SVN r16882/r16883: New deploy_controller.php (cache-clear endpoint)
Frontend Git: .gitlab-ci.yml updated with post-deploy curl for all 4 environments (commit 79dec94e, merged to develop)
GitLab CI variable DEPLOY_CACHE_TOKEN added to project settings
ALTER TABLE on 15 columns in aps_vendors (DATE to VARCHAR)
UPDATE queries to clean legacy 0000-00-00 values
Select a value from any dropdown column in Vendor Admin
Commits: Backend SVN: r16882, r16883 Frontend Git: SM-1032 branch, commits 67a5a1c6, 3f1028a2, 2d22b711, 79dec94e, merged to develop
Still need to test on Test. Moving to Stage Prep so we can push all the tickets at the same time.
Merged and Ready for Stage Testing\!
Verify once user chooses something in the drop down and refreshes the screen, that it saves.
Verify if user changes back to blank, that it saves it as blank and doesn’t revert back to what it was.
Verified once user chooses something in the drop down and refreshes the screen, that it saves.
Verified if user changes back to blank, that it saves it as blank and doesn’t revert back to what it was.
